market_summary: Mixed-to-risk-on regime remains intact for the 30-day horizon: SPY +12.24%, QQQ +16.88%, and IWM +12.67% over 1m, all above MA50/MA200, while TLT remains below both trend measures.

Leadership remains concentrated in AI/semicap and large-cap internet: KLAC, TSM, GOOGL, AVGO, NVDA, AMZN, and META all materially outperform SPY on 1m; COST is the slow, lower-volatility ballast and now sits below MA50.

No-go for rebalance today: weekly spacing discipline is binding, prior portfolio remains aligned with leadership, and qualitative refresh did not verify severe thesis damage sufficient to justify off-cycle turnover.

thesis_damage_flags.why: Pack shows weak relative momentum (1m +1.03% vs SPY +12.24%), below MA50, and recent qualitative follow-up is UNKNOWN; still not severe enough to override non-rebalance policy.
Strong 1m rebound (+27.70%) but still below MA200 with a deep 63d drawdown (-28.73%); qualitative refresh/estimate proxy is UNKNOWN, so thesis is improved but not fully de-risked.
Momentum remains strong, but qualitative refresh and key-ratio follow-up were UNKNOWN, so near-term earnings/guidance verification is incomplete rather than damaged.

thesis: Catalyst: no new severe negative qualitative evidence was verified in the refresh subset, while the existing book still matches the current AI/internet-led risk-on tape.

Factor edge: the standing portfolio already holds most of the strongest pack names on 1m/3m/composite momentum, so incremental expected alpha from an off-cycle change does not clearly exceed turnover drag.
Variant view: COST remains the weakest holding and would be the most likely future funding source on a scheduled rebalance, but today the evidence is insufficient to break cadence discipline.
risks: Macro: a rates shock or growth scare could reverse current tech/internet leadership and raise concentration risk in the existing book.
Idiosyncratic: qualitative/news verification is incomplete for several holdings, so undiscovered earnings or guidance deterioration remains a monitoring risk.
Execution/liquidity: forcing an unscheduled trade after strong recent gains in leadership names raises chase risk without a clear score-improvement hurdle.
falsifiable_checks: By the next scheduled rebalance checkpoint, if COST remains below MA50 and trails SPY on the rolling 1m window by 10 percentage points or more, treat it as an elevated replacement candidate.
Within 30 days, if two or more core leaders among NVDA, AVGO, KLAC, TSM, GOOGL, AMZN lose MA50 support and underperform SPY on the rolling 1m window, upgrade rebalance urgency from low to high.
trade_of_the_day.why_now: Base: regime is still favorable for the current quality-momentum book, so no-trade best preserves turnover efficiency. Bull: semicap and internet leadership persists, validating concentration in existing winners. Bear: leadership narrows or reverses and the defensive laggard COST continues to underperform. On a non-rebalance day, the expected edge from changing holdings does not clear trading-cost drag, so the paper candidate is to do nothing.

constraints_check.notes: Appendix: factor highlights=KLAC/TSM/GOOGL/AVGO lead on composite momentum; NVDA/AMZN remain strong; COST weakest and below MA50; qualitative fundamentals robust only for NVDA, others UNKNOWN. Risk budget=IT 53%, Comm Services 26%, no crypto, single-name max 15%, ballast via COST 8%. Scenario stress=equity index shock hurts book given growth concentration; rates shock negative for duration-like growth; commodity/USD shock limited direct exposure after XOM exit. Execution plan=no trade today per spacing rule; reassess at next scheduled checkpoint. Monitoring/kill-switch=watch COST relative weakness, META MA200 recovery failure, and any loss of MA50/relative momentum in two or more core leaders.
